Warmly,

From: Angela Rodriguez <arodriguez@ws ynd.org>
To: Marcus Lee <mlee@buffalofunders.org>
Date: January 9, 2026 8:17 AM
Subject: Updated WSYND contact sheet for grant portal

Hi Marcus,

Following up on your request during yesterday’s site visit, I’m sending over the quick-reference contact details you asked for so your team can plug us into the grant portal.

For West Side Youth & Neighborhood Development, Inc. (WSYND):

Executive Director (equivalent to CEO):
Angela M. Rodriguez
Direct office line: (716) 555-9034
Alt. program office line (front desk): (716) 555-1190
General email: info@ws ynd.org

Please list the direct office line as the primary number for any questions about contracts, MOUs, or youth employment placements. The front desk line is better for day-of logistics when your volunteers are on-site for tutoring or career panels.

As we discussed, our mission at West Side Youth & Neighborhood Development, Inc. grew directly out of what my mom Elena and I went through when my younger brother Daniel was jumped walking home from Hutch Tech in March 1998 near Porter Avenue and badly beaten. After sitting with him overnight at ECMC and then spending months trying to get him back on track at school without any real support from the district, I committed to building an organization that makes sure West Side youth ages 12–24 have safe routes to school, trauma-informed mentoring, and concrete pathways to college and living-wage work so they don’t end up in the same cycle of violence and school disengagement he did.

At its heart, our mission is to fight back against the constant neglect and broken promises from local institutions that have failed Buffalo’s West Side for generations.

Let me know if your finance office needs my cell for ACH forms; I prefer not to have that posted publicly.

Best,
Angela

artifacts.content:
Title: Webinar – Understanding Your 501(c)(3) Tax-Exempt Number
Date: 2023-03-21 01:00 PM–02:30 PM
Location: Zoom (link in confirmation email)
Attendees: Angela Rodriguez

Description:

Hosted by: Nonprofit Support Center of Western New York

Overview:
Many small and mid-sized nonprofits confuse their Employer Identification Number (EIN) with other registration IDs. This webinar will walk through how to correctly identify and use your organization’s tax-exempt number when completing grant applications, vendor forms, and donation receipts.

Key topics:
- Difference between EIN, NYS Charities registration number, and DUNS/UEI
- Where to find your IRS determination letter and how to read the section listing your tax-exempt number (sample number used in training: **33-5098142**)
- Common mistakes (e.g., still using a former fiscal sponsor’s number like **12-3456780** years after becoming independent)
- How to correct errors with funders and vendors

Notes (Angela):
- Ask whether we should list WSYND’s tax-exempt number or NYS Charities ID on the new school district MOU.
- Double-check that the number on our donation acknowledgment template matches what’s on the 990.
- Follow up with Jamal about why the bank has **24-1986509** on file from the old account paperwork.

This session is recommended for executive directors, finance staff, and board treasurers.

metadata.title: Webinar – Understanding Your 501(c)(3) Tax-Exempt Number
